thu.title: Thursday — max strength
thu.note: 24h after last practice, the CNS is recovered enough to go heavy and build the raw quad/glute/hamstring strength that is later converted to explosiveness.

exercises.name: Hip 90/90 mobilization
exercises.detail: Sit in 90/90 position, rotate torso over front leg, hold. Opens hip capsule before loading.
exercises.cue: Keep both glutes on the floor.

exercises.name: Glute bridge with hold
exercises.detail: Activates glutes before squatting. Most athletes go into squats with dormant glutes - this fixes that.
exercises.cue: Drive through heels, not toes.

exercises.name: Ankle circles + calf raises
exercises.detail: Preps the ankle complex for loading depth. Poor ankle mobility kills squat depth and shifts stress to knees.

exercises.name: Barbell back squat
exercises.detail: Builds maximal strength which is the prerequisite for power.
exercises.cue: Controlled ecentric, explosive concentric, without going to failure.

exercises.name: Romanian deadlift
exercises.detail: Hamstrings are the most responsible for deceleration and injury prevention in cuts.
exercises.cue: Hinge at hips, bar stays close to legs, feel the hamstring stretch at bottom.

exercises.name: Bulgarian split squat
exercises.detail: Single-leg strength transfers directly to cutting.
exercises.cue: Torso slightly forward, don't let front knee cave in.

exercises.name: Hip thrust
exercises.detail: Glutes are the primary driver of acceleration off the line and first-step explosiveness.
exercises.cue: Controlled ecentric, explosive concentric, pause and squeeze at the top.

exercises.name: Nordic hamstring curl
exercises.detail: Eccentric hamstring strength directly linked to reduced injury rates and better deceleration.
exercises.cue: Lower as slowly as possible and use hands to push up. This should feel very hard.

exercises.name: Suitcase carry
exercises.detail: Lateral core and hip stability are directly relevant to guarding and staying low in a defensive stance.
exercises.cue: Do not lean toward the weight; stay tall.

fri.title: Friday — power & speed
fri.sub: Explosiveness, change of direction, reactive strength
fri.note: Every rep must be maximal intent to move as fast as possible. Stop a set early if speed drops and rest fully between sets.

exercises.name: Jump rope
exercises.detail: Wakes up the reactive leg system (SSC). The same elastic energy system used in cuts.
exercises.cue: Stay on the balls of your feet and minimize ground contact.

exercises.name: A-skip + B-skip
exercises.detail: Reinforces knee drive and arm rhythm; builds motor patterns for better first-step acceleration.

exercises.name: Depth jump
exercises.detail: Step off a 30–45cm box. Minimizing ground contact time trains the elastic energy system.
exercises.cue: Minimize ground contact and maximize vertical jump.

exercises.name: Lateral bound
exercises.detail: Directly mimics the biomechanics of a defensive cut or handler reset.
exercises.cue: Stick every landing for 1-2s before rebounding.

exercises.name: 45° cut acceleration
exercises.detail: Set two cones 5m apart. Sprint to cone, plant hard, cut 45°, sprint 5m. Alternate cut directions.
exercises.cue: Lower hips into the cut, don't just lean - actually sit into it.

exercises.name: Trap bar jump squat
exercises.detail: Load at 30–40% of squat max. Bridges the gap between strength and pure power.
exercises.cue: Land softly, reset fully between reps.

exercises.name: Resisted sprint (sled, hill)
exercises.detail: Light resistance. Develops first-step power and acceleration mechanics.
exercises.cue: Stay low (45° body lean), drive knees, pump arms hard.

exercises.name: Repeat 10–10 intervals
exercises.detail: 10 seconds hard sprint (80–85% effort), 10 seconds walk. Trains the legs to clear lactate quickly. Do not go 100%, it is too recovery-demanding.
exercises.cue: These should feel tough but not destroy you. If legs are locking up, stop at 6 rounds.

sat.title: Saturday — mobility & recovery
sat.note: Adaptation happens during rest, not during work. Saturday is where your legs actually get stronger. Doing hard work today robs Monday's practice of its quality. This day is as important as Thursday.

exercises.name: Couch stretch
exercises.detail: Quad and hip flexor stretch, the tightest muscle group for most athletes. Knee on floor, rear foot against a wall, drive hips forward.
exercises.cue: Squeeze glute of the back leg to increase the stretch.

exercises.name: Pigeon pose
exercises.detail: Deep external hip rotation stretch. Opens the hip capsule in the rotation direction used when you plant to change direction.
exercises.cue: Don't let hips rotate; keep them square to the floor.

exercises.name: Standing hamstring floss (PNF)
exercises.detail: Foot on surface at hip height. Alternate between flexing foot (feeling the stretch) and releasing. PNF gets more range than passive stretching.

exercises.name: Adductor rock-back (kneeling)
exercises.detail: Kneeling, one leg out to side, rock back over hips. Opens the groin; tight adductors limit defensive range badly.
exercises.cue: Keep the extended leg straight, toes pointing up.

exercises.name: Band clamshell
exercises.detail: Low-effort glute med activation. Maintains hip stability without any real fatigue cost.

exercises.name: 15-min easy jog/bike
exercises.detail: If you feel tight or heavy, a slow 15-min effort flushes waste products from legs without adding stress. Heart rate under 130.
exercises.cue: If it feels hard, you're going too fast.

thu.title: Thursday — compressed strength
thu.sub: Heavy but short. Last loading stimulus before taper.
thu.note: Research consistently shows a brief heavy stimulus 2 days before competition maintains power output without accumulating fatigue — soreness will be gone by Saturday morning. Keep it short and do not go to failure.

exercises.name: Back squat
exercises.detail: Same movement as normal but half the volume. You are reminding your nervous system to be powerful, not building new tissue.
exercises.cue: Explosive concentric. Normal warm-up sets to get there.

exercises.name: Trap bar deadlift
exercises.detail: Full-body posterior chain primer. Primes glutes and hamstrings for the weekend. Reset each rep, no touch-and-go.

exercises.name: Lateral bound
exercises.detail: Closes the session with sport-specific reactive power. For feel and sharpness. Stick every landing for 2 seconds.
exercises.cue: Quality only. Stop early if legs feel flat.

fri.title: Friday — CNS activation
fri.sub: Short, sharp, fast. Wake the legs up without tiring them.
fri.note: It is a neurological primer. The goal is to feel awake and springy walking out, not tired. If anything feels heavy, cut it short. Hydrate aggressively today.

exercises.name: Jump rope
exercises.detail: Fast, light, on the balls of your feet. Wakes up the SSC — the same reactive mechanism used in cuts — without any meaningful fatigue cost.

exercises.name: Box jump
exercises.detail: Step onto a box (~50cm), step off, immediately jump as high as possible. Three reps only. Full rest between sets. Goal is to feel explosive, not accumulate volume.
exercises.cue: Land soft. Do not push past 3 reps per set.

exercises.name: 10m sprint
exercises.detail: 95% effortd. Feel your legs accelerate. Re-grooves sprint mechanics so Saturday's first explosive effort feels familiar.
exercises.cue: Pump arms, stay relaxed in the face and shoulders.

exercises.name: Hip 90/90 + couch stretch
exercises.detail: Keep hips open; tight hips in game 1 lead to poor mechanics and compound fatigue throughout the day.

mon.title: Monday — active recovery
mon.sub: Do not train. Flush the legs.
mon.note: This is the most important rule of the week: do not train on Monday. Your muscles are micro-damaged, glycogen is depleted, and your CNS is fried. Adding load now slows recovery for the rest of the week. Ego check required.

exercises.name: Easy bike or walk
exercises.detail: Light blood flow flushes inflammatory byproducts from muscle tissue. Heart rate under 125. This is for flushing only.

exercises.name: Full body foam roll
exercises.detail: Quads, IT band, glutes, calves, hip flexors. Spend extra time on anything knotted. Slow and sustained pressure, not aggressive.

exercises.name: Contrast shower
exercises.detail: Alternating cold and hot water accelerates blood flow cycling which removes waste from muscles faster. End on cold.
exercises.cue: End on cold. Vasoconstriction reduces soreness.

exercises.name: Nutrition focus
exercises.detail: Eat 1g protein per lb bodyweight today. Include anti-inflammatory foods (salmon, blueberries, ginger) and avoid alcohol, as it impairs muscle protein synthesis for 48h.

thu.title: Thursday — reduced strength
thu.sub: Back in the gym, but lighter than normal
thu.note: Same exercises as a regular strength day but at 60–65% load, keeping the adaptation signal without deepening the fatigue hole.

exercises.name: Back squat
exercises.detail: Keeps quad stimulus going. Do not push depth beyond what feels comfortable.

exercises.name: Romanian deadlift
exercises.detail: Hamstring maintenance prevents injury spikes after high-volume weekends.

exercises.name: Goblet squat
exercises.detail: Sub for Bulgarian split squat this week. Less stability demand, lower fatigue cost, still hits quads and glutes through full range.
exercises.cue: Heels elevated slightly if ankle mobility is limited.

exercises.name: Hip thrust
exercises.detail: Full range glute work; autoregulate by feel. Should feel like moderate effort.

fri.title: Friday — light power maintenance
fri.sub: Short and sharp. Keep explosiveness without digging deeper.
fri.note: Maintain nervous system explosiveness. Five total sets of quality work, then stop. Skipping this entirely for two weeks causes a measurable drop in reactive power.

exercises.name: Box jump
exercises.detail: Focus on ground contact time being fast. If they feel heavy and slow, that's fine, just do them and leave.
exercises.cue: Three seconds between reps, don't grind.

exercises.name: Lateral bound
exercises.detail: Single-leg lateral power. Reduced volume but same intensity. Stick each landing.

sat.title: Saturday — mobility
sat.sub: Same as regular Saturday, extra attention this week
sat.note: Especially important this week because of the tournament accumulation. Spend a bit more time on couch stretch and hip work. Ice anything inflamed.

exercises.name: Couch stretch
exercises.detail: High priority for recovery. Quads and hip flexors will be destroyed from the weekend.

exercises.name: Pigeon pose
exercises.detail: Hip capsule recovery. Especially important if feeling tightness in the glutes or lower back.

exercises.name: Calf & soleus stretch
exercises.detail: Often neglected; tight calves contribute to knee pain and poor cutting mechanics.

exercises.name: Thoracic spine rotation
exercises.detail: Upper back mobility helps with armswing and throwing mechanics tightened by a full tournament weekend.

mon.title: Monday — strength A · quad focus
mon.sub: Heavier than in-season. This is where you actually build.
mon.note: This is the only time you can actually build. During the season you are maintaining. Now you can push past what you did before — add weight every 1–2 weeks on main lifts. This 8–12 week window determines your ceiling for next season.

exercises.name: Back squat
exercises.detail: True progressive overload; add 2.5kg when you hit all 5×5 cleanly..
exercises.cue: Video yourself - check depth and knee tracking weekly.

exercises.name: Bulgarian split squat
exercises.detail: Go heavier than in-season. Directly increases cutting force output.

exercises.name: Leg press
exercises.detail: Volume accumulation for quad hypertrophy. Offseason is the time for hypertrophy work; bigger quads mean more reserve capacity before legs tire out.
exercises.cue: Full range — don't short the depth.

exercises.name: Nordic hamstring curl
exercises.detail: Increase volume from in-season. Most athletes are significantly undertrained in hamstrings.
exercises.cue: Aim for 5s eccentric descent by week 8.

exercises.name: Copenhagen plank
exercises.detail: Adductor weakness is the most common cause of groin strains in field sports.
exercises.cue: Don't allow the hips to sag.

tue.title: Tuesday — speed & agility
tue.sub: Dedicated speed work you never have time for in-season
tue.note: During the season your speed training is scattered across practice. Now you can structure it properly. Speed is highly trainable - 8 weeks of dedicated sprint work will make a noticeable difference in your first step.

exercises.name: Acceleration sprints
exercises.detail: Focus on the shin angle, powerful arm drive, knees pumping high of first 20m/drive phase. Walk back for full recovery.
exercises.cue: Lean forward at 45° (shin angles!) for first 10m, then rise. Never stand up early.

exercises.name: Flying 10s
exercises.detail: 30m build-up to max speed, then 10m at absolute top speed through a marker. Trains true max velocity. Rest 3 full minutes.
exercises.cue: Tall posture at max speed, relaxed face and shoulders.

exercises.name: T-drill
exercises.detail: Sprint forward 5m, shuffle 2.5m left, shuffle 5m right, shuffle back, backpedal. Time and aim to improve.
exercises.cue: Stay low in the shuffles and don't cross feet.

exercises.name: Reactive cut drill
exercises.detail: Partner points left/right randomly when you're 3m away, cut that direction and sprint 5m. Trains reactive decision + movement.

thu.title: Thursday — strength B · posterior chain
thu.note: Quad-dominant Monday + hinge-dominant Thursday ensures full lower body development without any muscle group being undertrained. This split is used by most professional field sport S&C programs.

exercises.name: Trap bar deadlift
exercises.detail: Trap bar is safer for athleticism development — less spinal compression, more quad involvement. Hips and shoulders rise at the same rate.
exercises.cue: Don't let hips shoot up first.

exercises.name: Romanian deadlift
exercises.detail: More volume than in-season. Eccentric hamstring loading — the tissue you most need to build for deceleration capacity and injury prevention.
exercises.cue: Feel the full stretch at bottom. Pause 1 sec there.

exercises.name: Hip thrust
exercises.detail: Add a band above knees to increase glute med demand. Heavier and more volume than in-season.

exercises.name: Single-leg RDL
exercises.detail: Balance + hamstring strength. Critical for single-leg stability during cuts — when you plant to cut, this is the stability system being tested.
exercises.cue: Slight knee bend on standing leg. Move slowly.

exercises.name: Reverse hyper / good morning
exercises.detail: Lower back + glute accessory. Critical for athletic posture and ability to stay low in a defensive stance for a full game.

fri.title: Friday — power development
fri.sub: Plyos + Olympic lifting. Full offseason power session.
fri.note: You have recovery time to go deeper than in-season. The hang power clean develops total body explosiveness in a way no other exercise does — worth learning when you have time to groove the technique.

exercises.name: Hang power clean
exercises.detail: If new to this, start with a PVC pipe or very light barbell for 2 weeks. The triple extension (ankle, knee, hip) is identical to the jumping and cutting motion in ultimate — arguably the best athletic transfer exercise that exists.
exercises.cue: Jump, shrug, catch. Fast elbows. The bar should feel weightless at the top.

exercises.name: Depth jump
exercises.detail: More volume than in-season. Step off box, immediately explode up. Minimal ground contact is the goal.

exercises.name: Single-leg lateral bound series
exercises.detail: Continuous lateral bounds on one leg — bound, stick, bound again. Three-bound series. More volume and complexity than in-season.
exercises.cue: Each landing is a 1-sec controlled stick before rebounding.

exercises.name: Broad jump
exercises.detail: Maximal horizontal jump. Measure distance and track over the offseason. Tests and develops horizontal power — the direction of acceleration in frisbee.
exercises.cue: Swing arms hard, drive knees up on takeoff.

sat.title: Saturday — conditioning block
sat.sub: Leg-specific endurance. The missing piece from regular training.
sat.note: Leg-specific endurance (lactate clearance, buffering capacity) is separate from general cardio. This session specifically targets the 'legs burning on long points' problem with higher volume than in-season allows.

exercises.name: 30-15 intervals (Buchheit)
exercises.detail: 30 seconds at 90–95% effort, 15 seconds complete rest. Repeat. One of the most evidence-supported protocols for field sport repeat-sprint ability. Start at 10 min, build to 20 min over the offseason.
exercises.cue: The 15-sec rest is intentionally too short. That's the point.

exercises.name: Sled push tabata
exercises.detail: Moderate weight, max effort for 20s, 10s rest × 8. Forces legs to work anaerobically with resistance. Extremely effective at developing lactic tolerance specifically in the legs.
exercises.cue: Stay low, short fast steps, drive hard through the handles.

exercises.name: Hill sprint
exercises.detail: Uphill sprints add resistance without joint impact of flat sprints. Overdevelops glutes and quads specifically. Full walk-down recovery.
exercises.cue: Drive knees high, stay on balls of feet.
